author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Valve overhauls tag system on Steam to improve game discoverability

Valve has updated its tagging system on Steam, introducing 17 new classifications, eliminating 28, and consolidating or renaming several others.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Fortnite returns to iOS worldwide, excluding Australia, as Epic prepares for "final battle" over App Store fees

Fortnite has returned to Apple's App Store worldwide, except in Australia, amid expectations that US federal courts will require Apple to disclose its fee structure.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
How Fireshine Games backed a winner with Far Far West

UK publisher Fireshine Games has scored a smash hit with the chaotic cyberpunk cowboy romp Far Far West, developed by French studio Evil Raptor. The game shifted 250,000 units in 48 hours after launching in Steam Early Access on April 28, going on to surpass the one million mark over the next two weeks.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Far Far West publisher says "We don't work with partners that are relying on generative AI"

Fireshine Games, the publisher behind Steam hit Far Far West, has said it won't work with developers that use generative AI art.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Gamecity Hamburg awards up to €80,000 to five games in latest Prototype Funding Round

Gamecity Hamburg has selected five digital games to receive up to €80,000 each in non-repayable grants from its latest Prototype Funding Round.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Original Doom soundtrack to be inducted into US National Recording Registry

The original Doom soundtrack will be added to the US National Recording Registry.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Sony announces PlayStation Plus price rises "due to ongoing market conditions"

Sony has confirmed price rises for its PlayStation Plus (PS+) subscription service.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Sony won't be bringing any more single-player games to PC, says PlayStation Studios boss

Following rumors earlier this year that Sony had decided to pull back from releasing PlayStation 5 exclusives on PC, PlayStation Studios boss Hermen Hulst has now reportedly told staff that PlayStation's single-player games will remain exclusive to its own consoles.

Read more
